## Summary

Fixes three production bugs reported after PRs #902 and #904 merged:
1. **Manuscript titles showing "Untitled Manuscript"** — manuscripts created via paste without a Project Title now auto-derive the title from the manuscript text and update the DB permanently on first evaluation report or workbench load.
2. **A/B/C revision variants are editorial instructions, not prose** — strengthened validation patterns to catch imperative editorial verbs, abstract beat-sheet language, and momentum/consequence formulations. Added explicit WRONG examples to the Pass 3 synthesis prompt showing the exact failure modes the LLM was producing.
3. **Revise queue "Diagnosis & Guardrails" missing on first load** — added `force-dynamic` to the workbench-v2 page to prevent Next.js from caching the server-rendered page.

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `lib/revision/reviseCardContract.ts` — expanded `FORBIDDEN_META_SUGGESTIONS` and `META_EDITORIAL_PATTERNS` with 20+ new entries targeting imperative verbs, abstract narrative-mechanics language, and LLM-typical editorial appendages
- `lib/evaluation/pipeline/prompts/pass3-synthesis.ts` — added 2 WRONG examples and 1 RIGHT example to the candidate_text prompt contract; added explicit "DO NOT append editorial commentary" instruction
- `lib/manuscripts/titleBackfill.ts` (new) — `backfillManuscriptTitleIfMissing()` loads manuscript text, derives title via `deriveManuscriptTitleFromText()`, updates DB
- `app/evaluate/[jobId]/page.tsx` — calls backfill when manuscript title is null
- `lib/revision/workbenchQueue.ts` — calls backfill in workbench title resolution chain
- `app/workbench-v2/page.tsx` — added `export const dynamic = "force-dynamic"`

**Does NOT touch:** scoring logic, test files, evaluation pipeline execution, database schema.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- **Title backfill** calls `getManuscriptText()` which reads from storage — adds one async read on first load for manuscripts with missing titles only. Subsequent loads use the DB-persisted title.
- **Validation tightening** may mark some previously-passing variants as `needs_targeting`. This is intentional — editorial instructions should never have passed validation.
- **force-dynamic** on workbench-v2 disables Next.js page caching. This is correct for a page that fetches user-specific auth + manuscript data.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — validation changes are display-side only; prompt changes affect future evaluations but do not alter scoring or pipeline flow.
